DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS' GENERAL DEMURRER OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SPECIAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIFTH AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Kristian Moriarty of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP (714.426.4600) for defendants and moving parties; Jennifer Spalding (707.254.5745) plaintiff and opposing party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff was granted final leave to amend the Fourth Amended Complaint to allege her claims in clear and concise language. Plaintiff has not done so. Plaintiff has been previously ordered to make factual allegations in support of each cause of action as to each defendant. Plaintiff has not done so. Instead, Plaintiff continues to allege general allegations of wrongdoing against all defendants without any differentiation. Plaintiff's apparent attempt to group all defendants together by alleging, generally, theories of joint and several liability and alter ego falls short. Plaintiff does not explain how any of the defendants are connected, who is an alter ego of whom, and fails to allege any facts showing a unity of interest and ownership or conduct amounting to bad faith. To be sure, its not even clear who these defendants are and how they are connected to this lawsuit.

Plaintiff's Fifth Amended Complaint fails to accomplish what the Court specifically instructed Plaintiff to do in this regard. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertions of "mere technical imperfections," Plaintiff's continued and consistent lack of specific allegations against any particular defendant renders the complaint uncertain, ambiguous, and vague such that the nine defendants cannot be expected to understand what allegations are being made against them and under which theories of liability. The policy of great liberality in permitting amendments is not without its limits. Defendants' demurrers to the First, Second, and Fourth Amended Complaint have been sustained with specific instructions to clearly identify the defendants against whom a particular cause of action is alleged against. Plaintiff has demonstrated that she has no intention of doing so. For this reason, the general demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT EMERALD FUND INC., EMERALD VAN NESS HAYES LLC, MARC BABSIN, 150 VAN NESS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES, LLC, SILVANUS ERICKSON DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
is SUSTAINED with a final leave to amend. Plaintiff has been previously ordered to allege her claims in clear and concise language. Plaintiff has not done so. Plaintiff has been previously ordered to make factual allegations in support of each cause of action as to each defendant. Plaintiff has not done so. Instead, the Complaint appears to be a compilation of citations to various laws, cases, and secondary sources combined with discussions of discovery motions in this case, and general allegations of wrongdoing by all defendants without little to no differentiation. This complaint is impossible to follow or respond to.
Plaintiff shall have 60 days to file an amended complaint. Plaintiff shall allege facts (1) in support of each element or (2) each cause of action; (3) as to each defendant. By way of an example, Plaintiff purports to assert a cause of action for breach of contract as to 11 listed defendants. As to each of these Defendants the Plaintiff must allege: (1) a contract and attach it, e.g. contract with Emerald Fund Inc; contract with Marc Babsin; contract with National Real Estate Development LLC, etc.); (2) facts showing that Plaintiff performed under each of the alleged contracts or is excused from performance; (3) each defendant breached their respective contract with the Plaintiff; and (4) resulting damages to the Plaintiff from that defendant's breach of that particular contract. Plaintiff shall refrain from citing cases (e.g. page 154 lines 12-19; page 160, lines 21-25), discussing legal concepts that are irrelevant to the particular cause of action (e.g. Intentional, knowing and fraudulent misrepresentation at page 154 line 16; concealment at page 155, line 22; strict liability of manufacturers at page 157, line 11-24; foreseeability, page 164, starting at line 11 ) and facts that have no apparent connection to the particular causes of action (see page 148, lines 15-17). The Court notes that the operative complaint is excessively long, repetitive, includes lengthy discussions of law and legal concepts, and does not tie whatever facts are being alleged to particular causes of action and particular defendants. Plaintiff shall attempt to make the complaint shorter, not longer. Plaintiff is reminded that complaint must be alleged in clear and concise language and must contain ultimate facts.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jennifer Spalding; 415-272-8043; Plaintiff, appearing in Pro Per.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions, legal authorities or general discussions) in support of each element of each cause of action. Plaintiff shall clearly label which cause of action is asserted against which defendant and include allegations pertinent to each specific cause of action as to each defendant against whom the cause of action is asserted. Complaint must be in clear and concise language. Plaintiff does not have leave to include any new causes of action. Plaintiff does not have leave to amend causes of action for strict liability and assault. If plaintiff wishes to assert these causes of action Plaintiff must file a motion for leave to amend. Plaintiff my use CACI as a guideline for elements of the causes of action. Late-filed oversized opposition is stricken. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of this order to file an amended complaint.
